topic: 2b.5 The 100% Dashboard — post-apr18 patches review (ISS-1103, ISS-1105 applied); first c1 against updated live-dashboard-bookkeeping block
change_summary: Decider applied two c1-derived patches to SEC-2b.5 in commit 5db9e5c (2026-04-18): (a) ISS-1103 added an H(r) first-principles caveat with a Section 1.8 crossref and softened 'two numerical parameters' to 'two numerical constants'; (b) ISS-1105 added a 2.8x domain-inflation sentence ('At 28 scored domains for 10 independent numerical knobs, the dashboard has a 2.8x domain-inflation factor relative to its underlying parameter count'). The decider's queue note explicitly asked for a new review file named SEC-2b.5-post-apr18.c1.json to avoid matching the stale SEC-2b.5.c1.json. The prior c1 review (2026-04-17T21:15:48Z) had 13 holes; patches in commits e51b1c5 and 5db9e5c addressed roughly 11 of those (holes 0-8 and 11-12). This c1 re-reviews the current post-apr18 content.
our_argument_summary: The post-patch SEC-2b.5 argues that the dome's public '100.0% / 28 of 28' live dashboard is label-inflation on top of six formulas and roughly ten independent numerical knobs — taking the dome author's own methodology.json and audit.html 'bookkeeping, not claim-by-claim audit' caveat at face value and performing the audit his document invites. After the ISS-1103 and ISS-1105 patches, the section (a) acknowledges the dome author's public framing of H(r)'s 8537/8619 as first-principles-derived before critiquing them as post-hoc calibrations via Section 1.8 crossref, (b) adds a 2.8x domain-inflation one-liner (28 dashboard labels / 10 independent knobs), (c) classifies the 28 into 14 STATIC + 6 BORROWED + 2 THEORY-FRAMEWORK + 3 LOOSE + 2 TAUTOLOGY-only + ~1 DISCRIMINATING, and (d) concludes the headline '100%' is carried by labels rather than independent confirmations. The overall argument is stronger than pre-patch, but the post-patch text introduces several new precision issues (inaccurate dome-author paraphrase, possibly imprecise parameter count, category-arithmetic mismatches).

tldr_review.notes: TLDR unchanged from c1. Still accurately frames the audit: dome's methodology.json warns the dashboard is 'bookkeeping, not a substitute for claim-by-claim audit,' we do the audit. TLDR compresses the classification to three buckets (six dome parameters, fourteen static re-references, a handful of borrowed globe values) — defensible at TLDR length. Note: the TLDR still says 'six dome parameters' while the post-patch body now uses 'two numerical constants' for H(r) and 'one parameter' / 'three parameters' for the other formulas. If the review is tightened per hole 1 below, the TLDR should also unify terminology (prefer 'numerical parameters' throughout). Severity: minor.

relevant_files: data/sections.json part2b html live-dashboard-bookkeeping subsection (15,210 chars total; 14,377 body chars; 629 TLDR chars) as of commit 5db9e5c (2026-04-18)

live fetch: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/formula_runtime.json (verified FORM-V51-001..006 and all cited constants 8537, 8619, 0.20, 14105, 1.672, 0.19550, 0.1640, 32.974, 20015, 0.66)
live fetch: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/methodology.json (verified claim_classes listing and bookkeeping caveat)
raw-text/05-model.txt (V12 KEY DISCOVERY: H(r) Reconciles three previously contradictory H measurements — Schumann 9,500 km, Polaris geometric 4,750 km, Model parameterization 9,086 km)
raw-text/01-home.txt ('derives predictions from first principles' — the only raw-text instance of 'first principles', stated as a general site claim rather than specifically about H(r) constants)
monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-172.json (source for the section; earlier domain_parameter_mapping_summary overlap disclosure)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Section audits the dome's published formulas and status_history.json entries against our classification rubric. The post-patch 2.8x inflation sentence is the numerical consequence of 28 domains / 10 independent knobs from formula_runtime.json plus three master geometric constants.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Section correctly characterizes the dashboard 'observed' column as hardcoded-equals-hardcoded for STATIC-win domains (monitor.py reads two identical values and reports 0% error). Matches our prior Part 2b findings. The new 2.8x inflation sentence is computed arithmetic (28/10).
data_sources_used: status_history.json (28 scored / 39 total domains, as of section-cited 2026-04-17)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Not applicable to this section — it is our audit of the dome's dashboard, not our monitor.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Section claims about formula_runtime.json content are exactly reproduced by live file (6 formulas, FORM-V51-001..006, parameter values listed match).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section-level prose item, not a WIN. No monitor.py automation targets this section content.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Section-level argument about dome practice, not a standard-physics relabel.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Our section is a response to dome changes made in April 2026 (live dashboard rollout, methodology.json, audit.html, and subsequent c1-derived patches). Timeline is reactive-to-dome-changes, not post-hoc in the dome-prediction sense.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Section argues about dashboard architecture — not derived from dome geometric parameters.

patches_from_c1_confirmed_applied.ISS_1103: Applied in commit 5db9e5c. Current text: 'two numerical constants (8537, 8619; the dome author derives these as aetheric pressure-scale values from first principles, but Section 1.8 shows them as post-hoc calibrations against observational data).' Crossref to #section-1-8 anchor is present and the anchor resolves. The lexical change 'parameters' -> 'constants' is applied to H(r) only; other formulas still use 'parameter' (see new hole 4).
patches_from_c1_confirmed_applied.ISS_1105: Applied in commit 5db9e5c. Current text: 'At 28 scored domains for 10 independent numerical knobs, the dashboard has a 2.8x domain-inflation factor relative to its underlying parameter count.' Math checks (28/10 = 2.8). Underlying '10 knobs' number may undercount the true parameter count (see new hole 3).

c1_holes_still_unresolved.description: Mascon Gravity STATIC entry still lacks the recommended range/point caveat. Current list entry reads 'Mascon Gravity ~26=28.1 mGal (WIN-076, 23% tol)' without acknowledging it is the outlier in the STATIC bucket (predicted is a range '~26', observed is a point 28.1 mGal, tolerance is atypically wide for the bucket). Recommended fix from c1 was an inline parenthetical.

Tesla Harmonic Series dismissal is still the brittle one-liner: 'If the fundamental exists, its harmonics exist as arithmetic.' A defender can reply that harmonics of a physical resonator are not pure arithmetic (each harmonic requires independent excitation/detection conditions). Recommended fix from c1 was to tighten to 'binary existence check, not a frequency-or-amplitude measurement,' but the text still reads 'as arithmetic.'

holes_found.description: New: the Section 1.8 crossref is imprecise. The patched text says Section 1.8 'shows them as post-hoc calibrations against observational data,' but Section 1.8 (Coordinate Collapse) does NOT contain the literal numbers 8537 or 8619, does NOT use the term 'post-hoc,' and does NOT use the word 'reconcile.' Section 1.8's argument is different (but adjacent): that the fitted H(r) describes the apparent rather than physical firmament height, because the Polaris elevations used to back-solve r are themselves refracted by the same n(r) medium. The stronger, more direct post-hoc-fit evidence is in the dome author's own raw-text/05-model.txt, which states H(r) 'Reconciles three previously contradictory H measurements' — Schumann resonance ~9,500 km, Polaris geometric 4,750 km, Model parameterization 9,086 km. A two-parameter exponential fit through three measured values is a textbook post-hoc calibration, and it is admitted by the author. A dome defender will click 'Section 1.8' expecting the H(r) post-hoc argument and find a different (legitimate, but different) argument.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the crossref with language that either (a) points to the actual admission: 'the dome author's own V12 log notes H(r) reconciles three previously contradictory H measurements (Schumann ~9,500 km, Polaris 4,750 km, model 9,086 km) — a two-parameter exponential fit through three observations is definitionally a post-hoc calibration,' or (b) keep the Section 1.8 crossref but reframe it: 'Section 1.8 shows the fit describes apparent rather than physical firmament height because the Polaris elevations it was calibrated from pass through the same n(r) medium.' Option (a) is preferred — it quotes the author against himself and strengthens the point.

holes_found.description: New: the phrase 'aetheric pressure-scale values from first principles' is not the dome author's actual framing. Raw-text search across 01-home.txt, 05-model.txt, 07-ai-context.txt, 08-live-power.txt finds the single phrase 'from first principles' only on the site home page as a general site-level claim ('derives predictions from first principles and tests them...'), not specifically about H(r). The phrase 'aetheric pressure-scale' or 'pressure scale' appears nowhere in raw-text for H(r). The dome author's actual framing for 8537/8619 is that H(r) is a 'V12 KEY DISCOVERY' that 'Reconciles three previously contradictory H measurements,' per 05-model.txt. Putting 'aetheric pressure-scale values' in his mouth makes our review look like it invented a charitable interpretation to argue against, rather than quoting the author's own words — and the author's own words are actually WEAKER for his position than the paraphrase we attribute to him.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'the dome author derives these as aetheric pressure-scale values from first principles' with something like 'the dome author's V12 discovery log presents H(r) = 8537 exp(-r/8619) as a function that reconciles three previously contradictory H measurements (Schumann resonance near the pole, Polaris geometric at mid-latitude, model parameterization at pole average)' — this uses his own framing, and the Section 1.8/post-hoc critique follows more forcefully because the admission is in the author's own words.

holes_found.description: New: the '2.8x domain-inflation factor' depends on the precise count '10 independent numerical knobs,' but an honest count of formula_runtime.json gives 12, not 10. Counting distinct numerical values: FORM-V51-001 contributes 8537, 8619 (2); FORM-V51-002 contributes 0.20 (1; 8537 is reused); FORM-V51-003 contributes 14105 (1); FORM-V51-004 contributes 1.672 (1); FORM-V51-005 contributes 0.19550, 0.1640, 32.974 (3); FORM-V51-006 contributes 0.66 (1; 14105 is reused; 20015 is a master). That is 9 formula knobs. Add master constants disc_radius 20015, sun_altitude 5733, moon_altitude 2534: total 12 independent dome numerical knobs. A dome defender will respond: 'Your inflation ratio is itself inflated — at 12 knobs the ratio is 28/12 = 2.33x, not 2.8x.' The earlier paragraph uses 'roughly ten,' which would absorb the imprecision, but the new one-liner states '10' and '2.8x' precisely.
holes_found.recommendation: Two paths: (a) State the count precisely as 12 (or more carefully, '~12 independent numerical knobs including master constants') and report 28/12 ~= 2.3x. This concedes the smaller ratio but is defensible on recount. (b) Keep 'roughly ten' and 'roughly 2.8x' consistently, and add a footnote: 'Counting the unique numerical constants in FORM-V51-001..006 plus the three master geometric constants yields nine to twelve independent knobs depending on whether reused values are counted once or per-occurrence; we use the lower-bound ten to stay conservative with respect to the dashboard's information content.' Option (b) is preferred — it preempts the 'your own number is wrong' counter-attack while preserving the quotable 2.8x. Either option, avoid leaving the precise '10' and '2.8x' as if they were tight measurements.

holes_found.description: New: internal inconsistency on 'parameter' vs 'constant' after the ISS-1103 lexical change. H(r) is now described as having 'two numerical constants (8537, 8619)' while the remaining five formulas are still described with 'one new parameter (0.20),' 'one parameter (14105),' 'one parameter (1.672),' 'three parameters (0.19550, 0.1640, 32.974),' and 'one new parameter (0.66).' If the intent of the ISS-1103 lexical change was to honor the dome author's first-principles framing specifically for H(r) (even while disputing it), the review should explain why the distinction applies only to H(r) and not to n(r)'s 0.20 or κ's 1.672. Otherwise a defender will ask: 'Why is 1.672 a parameter but 8537 a constant? Is this rhetorical concession only when you find it helpful?' The dome site publishes all six formulas as derived, not only H(r); applying the charitable term to one and not the others looks selective.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) apply 'numerical constants' consistently to all six formulas (since from the dome author's perspective, all are published as derived), with the first-principles-vs-fit caveat applied to the two that our review actually contests (H(r) and n(r)'s 0.20); or (b) revert ISS-1103's 'constants' back to 'parameters' for H(r) and instead put the first-principles caveat inline as 'the dome author presents these as first-principles derivations; Section 1.8 [or better, raw-text/05-model.txt V12 log] shows them as post-hoc calibrations.' Option (a) preserves ISS-1103 intent. Option (b) is simpler and eliminates the inconsistency.

holes_found.description: NMP Longitude category self-contradiction (was not in c1, or not sharpened in c1). NMP Longitude is listed as the third LOOSE item ('Loose / directional (3 domains)') with the bullet text 'Actually within 1% of observed. This is one of the few cells with real empirical content.' Then the next subsection ('The remaining genuinely-discriminating cell') identifies NMP Longitude as the one genuinely-discriminating cell: '...reduces to roughly one genuinely-discriminating cell: NMP Longitude (#3), where the dome's predicted 139.3 E falls within 1% of the observed 138.057 E on a 5% tolerance.' The LOOSE header criterion is 'tolerance so wide or criterion so directional that almost any observation passes.' A 5% tolerance with a 1% residual does not meet 'tolerance so wide.' NMP Longitude should be moved out of LOOSE and into a DISCRIMINATING bullet list. A defender will otherwise say: 'By your own rubric, your one remaining discriminating cell is LOOSE — so by your own tally, nothing discriminates.'
holes_found.recommendation: Move NMP Longitude out of the LOOSE list into a new 'Discriminating (1 domain)' bullet list preceding the 'The remaining genuinely-discriminating cell' prose section (or fold the 'discriminating' subsection into a bullet list). Update the LOOSE header to '(2 domains)': CMB Axis of Evil and NMP Drift Rate. Update the non-overlap tally from '3 LOOSE' to '2 LOOSE' and from '1 DISCRIMINATING' to '1 DISCRIMINATING' — the total is still 28 once the double count is removed (see hole 6).

holes_found.description: Non-overlap category-arithmetic mismatch on BORROWED. The tally sentence says '14 STATIC + 6 BORROWED + 2 THEORY-FRAMEWORK + 3 LOOSE + 2 TAUTOLOGY-only + 1 DISCRIMINATING = 28.' But Schumann Frequency is listed under Tesla-family and is explicitly labeled BORROWED in its bullet text. If Schumann is counted as BORROWED, BORROWED = 7, not 6. If Schumann is not counted as BORROWED, the Tesla-family has to contribute 3 unique items to the total (2 TAUTOLOGY + 1 Schumann). The current arithmetic only sums to 28 because NMP Longitude is double-counted (LOOSE + DISCRIMINATING). Once hole 5 is fixed (moving NMP Longitude to DISCRIMINATING only), the BORROWED undercount must also be fixed or the total will be 27.
holes_found.recommendation: Update the non-overlap tally to 14 STATIC + 7 BORROWED + 2 THEORY-FRAMEWORK + 2 LOOSE + 2 TAUTOLOGY-only + 1 DISCRIMINATING = 28. Also update the 'Borrowed from the standard model' header to '(6 domains)' if Schumann stays under Tesla-family, OR move Schumann to the Borrowed section and change its header to '(7 domains).' The Tesla-family grouping is structural ('driven by disc_radius and kappa'), so a Schumann entry belonging structurally to Tesla-family but classified BORROWED is defensible — the tally just needs to reflect it.

holes_found.description: Inherited from c1 hole 9: Mascon Gravity STATIC entry still lacks the range/point caveat. The list shows 'Mascon Gravity ~26=28.1 mGal (WIN-076, 23% tol)' where predicted is a range ('~26') and observed is a point (28.1 mGal), with a 23% tolerance that is unusually wide for the STATIC bucket (most STATIC tolerances are 0-10%).
holes_found.recommendation: Add parenthetical: '(Mascon Gravity is the outlier in the STATIC bucket — predicted is a range, observed is a point, and 23% tolerance is atypical; still classified STATIC because the source string is (WIN-076 static win) and no fresh data is fetched.)'

holes_found.description: Inherited from c1 hole 10: Tesla Harmonic Series dismissal is still 'If the fundamental exists, its harmonics exist as arithmetic' — attackable. Physical-harmonic content is not pure arithmetic; each harmonic requires independent excitation and detection conditions.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten to: 'STATIC (WIN-001 static win). The monitor test records Present vs Present with 0% tolerance — a binary existence check, not a frequency-or-amplitude measurement. In physical resonance, harmonics are not automatic arithmetic consequences of the fundamental; they require independent excitation and detection.'

holes_found.description: The new 2.8x sentence says 'its underlying parameter count,' using 'parameter' — but the adjacent H(r) line, after ISS-1103, uses 'constants.' Minor terminology drift that a careful reader will notice. See hole 4 for the broader issue.
holes_found.recommendation: Once hole 4 is resolved (unified 'parameters' or 'constants' throughout), this is naturally fixed. If keeping mixed usage, change '2.8x domain-inflation factor relative to its underlying parameter count' to '2.8x domain-inflation factor relative to its underlying knob count' (matches 'ten independent numerical knobs' earlier in the same paragraph).

holes_found.description: The citation '2.8x' is written as literal '2.8x' (with the times sign character) in the HTML. Fine for readers, but when quoted on social (SEC-1105 fix_details called it 'travels well on social'), the raw multiplication symbol U+00D7 may not render in some clients. Low risk, easy to pre-empt.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider writing 'approximately 2.8-fold' or 'roughly 2.8 times' in the main prose; keep '2.8x' only in places safe from client-encoding issues. Lowest-priority suggestion.

stronger_arguments.description: The dome author's methodology.json explicitly defines the claim class 'retrospective_structural' as 'An after-the-fact structural argument built from existing public data' with audit rule 'Judge the derivation quality and do not score it as a pure forecast.' This class fits H(r), n(r)'s 0.20, and the V13 eccentricity 0.66 almost verbatim. Our section could cite methodology.json directly: 'By the dome author's own taxonomy, H(r)'s 8537/8619, n(r)'s 0.20, and V13's 0.66 are retrospective_structural — fits to existing public data — not prospective forecasts. The author's own classification rule says do not score them as pure forecasts.' This is even stronger than the current crossref, because it uses the author's own published taxonomy against the dashboard's 100% framing.
sources: raw-text/05-model.txt (V12 KEY DISCOVERY reconciles three H measurements)

stronger_arguments.description: The 2.8x inflation argument can be strengthened by noting that real physics routinely has high knob-to-domain ratios too — General Relativity has one knob (G) and predicts planetary precession, light bending, gravitational waves, black hole shadows, and more. The discriminating distinction is not the ratio itself but whether each 'domain' provides independent empirical test. Our section already makes this point (the 14 STATIC bucket shows most of the 28 aren't independent tests), but could be sharpened: 'A low-parameter model that predicts many independent phenomena is good physics. A low-parameter model whose dashboard lists the same phenomena 14 times as separate domains is not. The 2.8x domain-inflation factor is a symptom of the latter, confirmed by the bucket analysis.'

kernel_of_truth.description: Post-patch, SEC-2b.5 is a stronger section than pre-patch. ISS-1103 correctly acknowledges the dome author's public framing (that H(r) is a derivation, not a fit) before disputing it — this is the right credibility move. ISS-1105's 2.8x inflation sentence is quotable, travels well on social, and is arithmetically honest (modulo the 10-vs-12 knob-count question in hole 3). The section performs exactly the audit that the dome author's own methodology.json invites — the strongest possible rhetorical posture. The dome author's methodology.json and audit.html additions are real methodological improvements that we credit in the Kernel of truth block; that credit is intact after the patches.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The patches address c1 holes but introduce new precision issues. Most importantly, the paraphrase of the dome author's framing ('aetheric pressure-scale values from first principles') is not in his raw materials, and the Section 1.8 crossref points to an adjacent but not identical argument. These are fixable in a minor_edit pass — the overall argument is sound, just needs a tighter citation chain.

deeper_analysis: Key adversarial concern: the ISS-1103 patch strengthened the section rhetorically (acknowledging the author's framing before disputing) but weakened its citation chain (the author's actual raw-text framing is different from the paraphrase, and Section 1.8 doesn't contain the post-hoc argument we attribute to it). A sharp dome defender can do exactly what we do in adversarial mode: click through the crossref, note that Section 1.8 argues something different, and accuse us of invoking a reference that doesn't contain the claimed argument. The fix is cheap — use the dome author's own V12 log as the citation, with Section 1.8 as supporting context. This flips the critique: instead of 'we say Section 1.8 shows them as post-hoc; it doesn't quite,' we get 'the author's own V12 log says H(r) reconciles three previously contradictory H measurements — this is a post-hoc two-parameter fit through three data points by the author's own admission.' That argument is bulletproof because it quotes the author verbatim. Second adversarial concern: the 2.8x / 10-knob arithmetic is a high-visibility metric likely to be quoted on social and in discussions. If it can be recomputed to 2.33x by a motivated defender, the quote becomes a liability rather than an asset. Easy fix: 'roughly 2.8x' or a footnote acknowledging the 9-12 knob range. Third concern: NMP Longitude category self-contradiction is an old bug preserved through the patches — it existed in c1 (one version of EXP-172 and the integrated section) but was not flagged specifically because c1 focused on Aetheric Redshift Scale. Moving NMP Longitude out of LOOSE and into an explicit DISCRIMINATING list is the cleanest fix; it also resolves the '6 vs 7 BORROWED' tally issue once the double count is eliminated.

citations_verified: formula_runtime.json FORM-V51-001..006 and the constants 8537, 8619, 0.20, 14105, 1.672, 0.19550, 0.1640, 32.974, 20015, 0.66 — all match live fetch
methodology.json 'bookkeeping, not a substitute for claim-by-claim audit' quote — matches live fetch
methodology.json claim_classes enumeration (prospective_confirmed, retrospective_structural, supportive_nonunique, pending_contested, open_refinement) — matches live fetch
audit.html 'bookkeeping for the current registry state' quote — matches live fetch
#section-1-8 anchor in part1 — resolves to Section 1.8 (Coordinate Collapse) heading at index 63481 in part1.html
citations_failed: 'Section 1.8 shows them as post-hoc calibrations against observational data' — Section 1.8 does NOT contain '8537,' '8619,' 'post-hoc,' or 'reconcile.' It makes the adjacent argument that n(r) contaminates angular measurements used to back-solve r. The claimed argument (H(r) parameters are post-hoc fits) is in the dome author's raw-text/05-model.txt V12 KEY DISCOVERY log, not in our Section 1.8. This is the hole-1 citation issue.

'the dome author derives these as aetheric pressure-scale values from first principles' — no raw-text instance of 'aetheric pressure-scale' or 'pressure scale' tied to H(r). The only 'first principles' usage is a site-level home-page sentence, not specifically about H(r). This is the hole-2 citation issue.
citations_unchecked: status_history.json row indices 27 and 33 for Roaring 40s Wind Speed and SAA-Roaring 40s Boundary — inherited from c1 where it was verified; not rechecked here
